summary: Error message when adding a new entry with an escaped opening curly bracket should be improved.

description.text:
**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**
When creating a new BibTeX entry in JabRef where an author has an umlaut in their name, it might happen that accidentally, the opening curly bracket is escaped instead of the parenthesis. This results in an exception that the file could not be saved. Unfortunately, the stack trace is directly shown without highlighting the character that resulted in the exception (in this case, the closing curly bracket would be falsely required by the stack trace to be escaped too). 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Highlighting the character in the stack trace helps quickly identify the issue.

summary: Tag-Based Selection for Entry fields in Preferences

description.text:
This PR Fixes #12550

This PR replaces the existing text-based field selection with a tag-based selection format, similar to the implementation in the Autocompletion settings.

### Changes Made

- Converted text-based text input to TagsField components, allowing users to select fields dynamically instead of relying on static text labels.
- Improved layout alignment to ensure text and tags are displayed correctly.
- Adjusted spacing and padding in HBox to prevent label truncation or misalignment.
- Ensured that the new TagsField selection method works consistently with other settings sections.

### Screenshots after Fix Applied
![new](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d62ee062-013f-4247-8be6-a6c459c037c6)


### Mandatory checks
- [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE)
- [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user)
- [ ] Tests created for changes (if applicable)
- [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required)
- [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes)

summary: auto rename feature  issue-11316

description.text: UI:Add option to disable automatic file renaming on entry changes This PR adds a new user preference option to control whether files should be automatically renamed when entry details change: (below the linked file name convention is the button I added, auto rename files if entry data changed) FilesOnEntryChange (default: false) •Added new boolean preference in FilePreferences: autoRename •Added checkbox in Preferences > Linked Files settings to toggle this option •Modified AutomaticFileRenamer to respect this preference setting •Updated all relevant tests to accommodate this new preference This change gives users more control over when files are renamed, addressing concerns about unexpected file system changes. Users are now able to decide whether they want to automatically change their linked file name when they change their entry data. LOGIC: JabRef can automatically renames linked files when entry data changes through an event-based system. Core implementation: AutomaticFileRenamer: Listens for entry changes, checks user preference before renaming  EnhancedFileRenamer: Executes renaming with pattern-based filenames FilePreferences: Stores the new preference setting (default: off) Key tests: AutomaticFileRenamerTest: Tests citation key change renaming, field change behavior EnhancedFileRenamerTest: Tests filename generation, conflict handling LinkedFileHandlerTest: Tests linked file operations with preference This feature maintains synchronization between bibliographic entries and filenames while giving users control over the automatic behavior. ps: for the case that multi linked files are linked to an entry, the current solution is to if the user change the entry data, all the linked files will change.  Closes [#11316](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11316) ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

summary: Fix for issue 11316

description.text: UI:Add option to disable automatic file renaming on entry changes This PR adds a new user preference option to control whether files should be automatically renamed when entry details change: (below the linked file name convention is the button I added, auto rename files if entry data changed) FilesOnEntryChange (default: false) - Added new boolean preference in FilePreferences: autoRename - Added checkbox in Preferences > Linked Files settings to toggle this option - Modified AutomaticFileRenamer to respect this preference setting - Updated all relevant tests to accommodate this new preference This change gives users more control over when files are renamed, addressing concerns about unexpected file system changes. Users are now able to decide whether they want to automatically change their linked file name when they change their entry data. LOGIC: JabRef can automatically renames linked files when entry data changes through an event-based system. Core implementation: AutomaticFileRenamer: Listens for entry changes, checks user preference before renaming EnhancedFileRenamer: Executes renaming with pattern-based filenames FilePreferences: Stores the new preference setting (default: off) Key tests: AutomaticFileRenamerTest: Tests citation key change renaming, field change behavior EnhancedFileRenamerTest: Tests filename generation, conflict handling LinkedFileHandlerTest: Tests linked file operations with preference This feature maintains synchronization between bibliographic entries and filenames while giving users control over the automatic behavior. ps: for the case that multi linked files are linked to an entry, the current solution is to if the user change the entry data, all the linked files will change. Closes #11316 ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I licence it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (for UI changes) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? summary: When merging groups or keywords, remove duplicates

description.text:
<!--
Please use the GitHub issue tracker only for bug reports and smaller suggestions for improvements.
Requests for completely new features, questions and general feedback is now handled at https://discourse.jabref.org.
Thanks!
-->

**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**

In the Merge dialog, JabRef merges groups or keywords by stringing the field values together from two entries. This can create duplicates in the merged entry

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/262fc176-1e46-4ca1-984e-226689077f96)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0fba4f9b-0862-45ef-bb65-019d5653f4ea)

**Describe the solution you'd like**

When merging groups or keywords automatically, remove exact duplicates (case sensitive) from the list. Implementing this suggestion would also fix #12535 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70b5e7a1-045f-42dd-82ef-5468aa5ca70d)

De-duplication of groups and keywords could also be used as a quality check.

**Additional context**

/src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/mergeentries/newmergedialog/fieldsmerger/GroupMerger.java
/src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/mergeentries/newmergedialog/fieldsmerger/KeywordMerger.java

summary: Have the context menu of linked files working for multipe selected files.

description.text:
Currently, the functionality of "Remove link" is working for one file only. That means, deletion of multiple linked files with "Remove link" is not possible.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ca3e363-3c7e-4e5a-8234-5726b3d3d49a)

## Task

Rework all entries of the context menu being either disabled for multiple selected files or work with multiple selected files.

| Current | New | Comment |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Edit | (disabled) | |
| Open file | Open file(s) | iterate through all selected files and open each |
| Open folder | Open folder(s) | collect all folders of all selected files (in a set) and open each |
| Download file | Download file(s) | |
| Rename file to a defined pattern | (disabled) | Disabled, because there is a dialog chec|
| Move file to file directory | Move file(s) to file directory | enabled only if **all** files are outside the file directory |
| Copy linked file to folder... | Copy linked file(s) to folder... | All files are copied to the selected destination folder |
| Redownload file | Redownload file(s) | only enabled if at least one file has an online link |
| Remove link | Remove link(s) | |
| Permanently delete local file | Permanently delete local file(s) | |

Note that the localization strings need to be replaced.

## Illustration
 
See https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12565 at "Current behavior" how to attach two files to an entry.

## Hints

### Auto link feature

5. Start JabRef
6. Open `test-library-with-attached-files.bib` contained in `src\test\resources\org\jabref\logic\search`.
7. Delete all entries EXCEPT `minimal-sentence-case`. "Keep file(s)" while deleting!
8. Select entry `minimal-sentence-case`
9. Go to tab "General"
10. See two files attached
11. Select both
12. "Remove link"
13. Both should be gone (above)
14. Switch entries
15. Switch back
16. See that the two links are up again.

This is OK (!) because the entry editor automatically links files beginning with the citation key

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b48dc865-d45e-4f46-b8ed-7fced5658ecd)

summary: Make strings consistent

description.text:
There are two functionalities for renamign attached files.

In the entry editor

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/210cf185-36ae-481b-b3ff-095bd704254e)

in "Quality" > "Cleanup entries"

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3a98017-b1e4-4e65-ad54-563291ed1e18)

The text of both menu entries should be the same. It needs to be changed to

> Rename files to configured filename format pattern

Change "Filename format pattern:" to "Filename format pattern (from preferences)"

Also change "Rename only PDFs having a relative path" to "Rename files having a relative path only"

See https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12565 for a step-by-step instruction for triggering rename of a single file in the entry editor.

---

Hint: https://devdocs.jabref.org/code-howtos/localization.html

Related: https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11368

summary: Refine "File exists" warning

description.text:
The current "error" dialog for renaming a file to an existing file is not good.

Improvements:

1. Allow "Keep both" -> JabRef adds ` (1)`, ` (2)`, ... at the file name. 
    * Use `org.jabref.logic.util.io.FileNameUniqueness#getNonOverWritingFileName` for this
    * Hover on the button "Keep both": Shows tool tip "New filename {}", with {} replaced by a filename not existing yet. Example: "Bogner (1).pdf" if "Bogner.pdf" would be generated and exist.
2. Add button "Provide alternative file name", which triggers the functionality "Rename file to a given name" from the context menu having the proposed name in the text box
3. Change title from "File exists" to "Target file already exists"
5. The content of the message box is strange for a long file name. Content: "Target file name {}"
6. Content of message box should not be editable.
---

Current behavior:

1. Open `pdfContentImporterTest-pdfs.bib` (contained in `src\test\resources\pdfs\PdfContentImporter`)
2. Remove "Tucker2007" entry, but "Keep file(s)"
3. Select "Bogner2023RESTAPI"
4. In the Windows Explorer, select `Tucker2007.pdf`. Drag it onto "Bogner2023 RESTAPI" in the main table
5. In the "General" tab in the entry editor at the field "File", there are now two entries
6. Select the second one
7. Context menu (right click)
8. Select "Rename file to defined pattern"

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/210cf185-36ae-481b-b3ff-095bd704254e)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b980484-9978-4d3a-bc19-71f41183d85d)


(Internal discussion: We could have an "Advanced hint" explaining all options in the dialog box, but we think this is not necessary to understand the actions taken by JabRef)

summary: Drag'n'drop of PDF does nothing

description.text:
## Illustration
 
1. Start JabRef
7. Open `test-library-with-attached-files.bib` contained in `src\test\resources\org\jabref\logic\search`.
8. Select entry `minimal-sentence-case`
1. Change citation key to "x"
2. Navigate to tab "General"
3. Drag and drop "minimal-note-all-upper-case.pdf" to the "File" box
4. See that nothing happens

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b09edbc2-539b-4b2e-b0f7-3e788c7b15d7)

Expected behavior: 

- File already linked: There should be a notification (`dialogservice.notify(...)`) that the file is already linked in the library and thus is not relinked
- File not already linked: Link the file

**Note:** This is kind of detective work

Drag and drop onto the entry in the main table works.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/345d9647-6470-4089-b2a4-dbd5ff9c93c5)

summary: Replace "Download linked online files" by "Download references files (PDFs, ...)"

description.text:
JabRef has a preference for downloading referenced files:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb4448af-d436-41df-97c6-668d3e3c3c57)

The text is somewhat misleading.

It says that with this checkbox, JabRef downloads a file that is directly linked in the search engine response (and JabRef does not attempt to find a full-text article).

## Tasks

1. Change this text to

    > Download referenced files (PDFs, ...)

2. Update documentation https://docs.jabref.org/collect/import-using-online-bibliographic-database

## Hints

- https://devdocs.jabref.org/code-howtos/localization.html
- Repository of documentation: https://github.com/JabRef/user-documentation

summary: Enforce saving of library before (relative) file paths are used

description.text:
1. Start JabRef using `./gradlew run` (let that directory be `X`)
1. Create new library
2. Do not save
3. Open Library properties: "Library-specific file directory". See that this can be changed.

Proposed workaround:

1. Preferences
    1. Make all three library directory settings field read-only\
        ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/56fcb3b3-1371-4a21-9ad2-15355c192d93)
    2. Add text "Save library first" below these fields
3. When a user drops a PDF file onto JabRef, it should not be allowed to the user (Windows should show crossed icon)



---

Related: https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12556, https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12558, and https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11920

summary: Disable "Search for unlinked local files" for new library

description.text:
1. Start JabRef
2. Create new library
3. DO NOT SAVE
4. Open "Lookup" menu
5. See that "Search for unlinked local files" is clickable

The action "Search for unlinked local files" should only run if the current library is saved (so that the path of the .bib file is known).

---

**Hint:** Look at https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12199/files for code hints.

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11920

summary: Fix file renaming

description.text:
1. Start JabRef
2. Check preferences that the citation key pattern for files is `[bibtexkey] - [title]`\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f115bf9c-53a1-4e0c-9612-fdaf58215ff6)
3. Create new library
4. Save library to folder `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
5. Drag an drop file  `Bogner2023.pdf` from `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
6. Go to tab "General"
7. Scroll down to "File"
8. Right click and select"Edit" \
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa7e9714-8b36-4d0e-840c-5649ef2a760b)
9. See that filename misses citatoin key\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/930b8def-1eb0-4f0a-8432-1e7dcba8467b)
10. Select "Quality" > "Cleanup entries"
11. Ensure that "Rename PDFs to given filename format pattern" is checked.
12. Click OK\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c82ce7d2-da3c-467a-8db0-af60d724080b)
13. In the warning, click "Autogenerate PDF Names"\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/edfc55c2-4689-468c-94a7-de23a24bbdac)
14. Click on "Edit" again. See that citation key is  in:\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7fd68e2-5332-4c1e-896c-a842fb895b32)

Wish: File should have correct name after import.

Task:

1. Understand what happens at drag and drop
2. Ensure that citaton key generation happens before file name renaming

If 2 is not possible, report back, why.


---

summary: Save the last directory from which files were added

description.text:
<!--
Please use the GitHub issue tracker only for bug reports and smaller suggestions for improvements.
Requests for completely new features, questions and general feedback is now handled at http://discourse.jabref.org.
Thanks!
-->

**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**
In 99.9% of all cases, I add files to an entry from my downloads folder. However, JabRef always opens the file selection dialog in the library's folder.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would find it easier if the folder that was used last is opened again. Ideally, this should be a setting that is remembered over restarts of JabRef.

**Additional context**

summary: Add markdown as copy format

description.text:
## Background

### Maintable

The current context menu of a main table entry contains two copy preview options:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1738a19f-5d4d-4eca-8641-4ffa6ab53dfb)

### Preview Area

The context menu of the preview only offers copying HTML:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/860d698f-9205-4c63-b8a8-e2dc7ab9bc86)

## Task

1. Add "Copy citation (Markdown) as context menu entry to main table context menu
2. Add "Copy citation (Markdown) as context menu entry to preview panel
3. Add `CSL.setConvertLinks()` to CSL configuration (stated at https://github.com/michel-kraemer/citeproc-java/issues/252#issuecomment-2511285869)

## Note

- This task is related to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12551. It can be done before or after the other task. If done in parallel, there will be some merge conflicts (but propbaly not too large)
- This is a follow-up to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12220

summary: Use a Tag Field for Resolve strings and Do not wrap under Preferences -> Entry for

description.text:
Currently, it's not easy to see and distinguish the BIbTeX fields. It should use a TagField like in Autocompletion.

### Current implementation

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ca5f396-8716-4173-98ff-ea83e66ee366)

### Desired look and feel:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b989b9b3-1c1b-4c12-a7eb-99988b37610d)

### Task

1. Analyze how "Autocompletion" preferences works
2. Port to "Entry > Affected fields" and "Entry > Do not wrap when saving"

Do not modify the preference object itself; "just" the UI

summary: When fetching from DOI: Dialog should select field data based on validity

description.text:
As user, I can refine entry's data using a DOI. See https://docs.jabref.org/finding-sorting-and-cleaning-entries/getbibtexdatafromdoi#completing-information-based-on-doi-or-isbn for an example.

Then, for any field, it could be possible that information is existing locally ("Original entry") and from the DOI ("Entry from DOI").

Example:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30b445ac-c3e7-440f-a9da-6fdcdf36f616)

However, sometimes, the local data is really wrong. In this example, the "year" (`1109`) and the "Entrytype" (`Misc`).

The dialog should select the data which could be more right. Here, "Entrytype" and "Year" should be selected from "Entry from DOI":

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c866899-9745-4b55-852d-dd1d8b27bfe4)

### Task

1. Find out how the Merge Entries Dialog is called
2. Modify it to know that it knows that is called from "get bibliographic data from DOI" (or not)
3. If called from "get bibliographic data from DOI", then it should go through all fields existing in both entries and select the ones more "right". Implement it for entry type (misc; != misc) and year. - Also use "DOI" from the "DOI" fetcher (if code-wise clean enough)
4. Implement a class "FieldValueValidityComparator" implementing a comparator. The score returned is -, +, 0 depending if the left,, right value are "better" and 0 if the fields are equal or the comparator cannot tell
5. Inherit from it for `YearFieldValueValidityComparator`.
6. Use these comparators in the Merge Entries Dialog

### Details

The dialog currently prioritizes the entry from the library. Sometimes, the data from the library is really wrong.

Example:

```bibtex
@Misc{,
  year      = {1109},
  doi       = {10.1109/IPDPSW55747.2022.00093},
  publisher = {IEEE},
}
```

1. Wrong entry type
2. Wrong year

When the merge entries dialog is started, it should select the more propriate entry type (everything else as MISC is better) and the better year (if year differs more than 10 years, the "new" year should be taken)

summary: Web import: New entry should be added to group "Imported entries"

description.text:
As user, I want to know which entries I did import the last times. It is possible to check for the last added entry (https://docs.jabref.org/advanced/entryeditor/timestamp).

After an import, the last imported entry is highlighted. If I import multiple entries, only the last one is highlighted. If I repeatedly import entries.

As user, I have two phases: Searching and importing entries; and clean up of entries. For cleanup, I need to know which entries need clean up. I mix between web search and PDF import. 

After an import, some tweaks in the bibliographic entries need to be done: Fix of authors, fix of titles. E.g., by applying [Integrity Checks](https://docs.jabref.org/finding-sorting-and-cleaning-entries/checkintegrity). Not all of them are "right" and need to be manually checked.

Another use case for marking imported entries is that they are kind of papers one wants to read.

Therefore, JabRef should automatically add entries to a group "Imported entries".

### Desired feature

- After web search, new entries should be added to the group "Imported entries". The group name can be changed in the preferences:
- After a PDF is imported, it should also be added to this group
- After importing from DOI or other web fetchers, it should also be added to this group
- The feature should only be enabled if enabled in the preferences
- The name of the group should be (globally) configurable

#### Details

Enable configuring the group to add to:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8de9c546-8815-4f32-baec-7c0f04a6877b)

### Code hints

- `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.ImportHandler#importEntryWithDuplicateCheck(org.jabref.model.database.BibDatabaseContext, org.jabref.model.entry.BibEntry, org.jabref.gui.duplicationFinder.DuplicateResolverDialog.DuplicateResolverResult)`
- Store `% Imported entries` in the preferences. 
  - In the preference defaults, store `"% " + Localization.lang("Imported entries")`
  -   When getting the preference, check whether the string begins with `%`. If yes. Remove `%` and the space after and lookup using `Localization.lang`.

---

### References

- Feature wish in the forum: https://discourse.jabref.org/t/mark-entries-imported-into-an-existing-database/1988
- Description of functionality: https://discourse.jabref.org/t/imported-entries-stay-on-top/189/2
- ADR to use `%` for default strings https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/11988
- In JabRef 2.x, wie had a "marker" for imported entries
- Similar feature: Mark and unmark of entries in JabRef: https://web.archive.org/web/20160614075506/https://help.jabref.org/en/MarkingHelp

summary: Add Filetype "mp4" in file links

description.text:
<!--
Please use the GitHub issue tracker only for bug reports and smaller suggestions for improvements.
Requests for completely new features, questions and general feedback is now handled at http://discourse.jabref.org.
Thanks!
-->

**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**
Some articles have videos as supplementary information. Files with an mp4-extension are not recognized in the file-field and have to be added manually (e.g. by setting the filetype to "JPG image" in the "Edit file link" window and manually searching for the videos path). Afterwards, the video is played without problems via the default program by simply clicking on the file link.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Include mp4 extension in files.

summary: Drag'n'drop of a PDF (from file explorer) to group should add the file

description.text:
1. Open Windows Explorer
2. Navigate into folder `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
3. Open JabRef
4. Create new library
5. Save library to folder `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
6. In the group view on the left: Click on "Add group" 
7. Enter "test" as name
8. Click on "OK"
9. If no group is added, repeat steps 5 to 7 (another issue in JabRef)
10. Drag `Baldoni2002.pdf` onto JabRef table
11. Result: PDF added
12. Drag `Bogner2019.pdf` onto group "test"
13. After dropping: nothing happens

Expected behavior:

1. PDF is imported as in step 10
2. PDF is added to group test

Hint: Method `org.jabref.gui.maintable.MainTable#handleOnDragDroppedTableView` is handling the drop on the main table.

summary: Fix Citationkey pattern dropdown UI issue

description.text:

Issue: The added dropdown has an issue, it shifts upwards when it reaches the bottom of the screen due to the default behavior of Context Menu in JavaFX.

This PR ensures that the dropdown is properly positioned and dynamically adjusts its height based on the available space. 

Additionally, the "All patterns" submenu is always shown when displaying the suggestions list is not possible, ensuring that the  UX is not compromised.

summary: User-defined keyword separator does not apply to Merge Groups

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

openSUSE Tumbleweed

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

The user-defined "Keyword separator" applies to keywords and groups in the entry table, but not in the Merge/Find duplicates window. The correct separator is used to merge keywords, but not groups.
 
1. Set a non-default keyword separator in Preferences > Entry
2. Notice that the keyword separator also applies to the `groups` field (as intended)
3. Create two entries with at least one group per entry but not identical group sets.
4. Select the two entries and click Quality > Merge Entries
6. In the merge window, Click the icon for `Merge Groups`
7. Notice that the groups are joined using the default separator (comma) instead of the user-defined character.

The keyword separator is applies to `keywords` and `groups`, so I expect the same separator to be used for merging keywords or merging groups.

### Appendix

JabRef 100.0.0
Linux 6.12.8-2-default amd64 
Java 23.0.1 
JavaFX 23.0.2+3
...

<summary>Log File</summary>

```
Not applicable
```

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

I wonder whether the issue relates to wayland, gnome or fedora linux..., based on our discussion at the [forum](https://discourse.jabref.org/t/external-programs-custom-terminal/5418/2).


2. in the JabRef settings, specify a custom terminal call ` /usr/bin/gnome-terminal --working-directory='%DIR'`, accept
3. back in the main window, use Menu tools/open terminal here.

In my case, JabRef seems to resolve `'%DIR'` correctly (I can copy the event log's command and paste it in the terminal manually, and there the call is effective), but no terminal appears on my gnome desktop, neither in the background nor in the foreground.

The quotes `'...'` are necessary for my terminal to handle the directory path, which includes blanks...

### Appendix

_No response_

summary: Preferences: Add manual prompt control for cross-references in Copy To

description.text:
This adds an option to "General->User Interface" to manually enable/disable the prompt that asks the user if they would like to include cross-references when using "Copy to". 

This is primarily to allow users to re-enable the prompts if they have disabled them from within the prompt.

Closes #12501

summary: Fix linked files tooltip to display all files on hover

description.text:
Fixes #12470 

this PR fixes hovering over the Linked Files tab, the tooltip now displays all the files linked

Updated getDescriptionAndLink() to return all linked files' descriptions and links.

Previously, the tooltip was set using .withStringTooltip(LinkedFileViewModel::getDescriptionAndLink), which only fetched one file.

updated it to fetch all linked files using the newly created method.

The method createFileTooltip() was only picking the first file’s link.

updated it to iterate over all files and join them using newline characters

summary: Read Status - Extra Options

description.text:
<!--
Please use the GitHub issue tracker only for bug reports and smaller suggestions for improvements.
Requests for completely new features, questions and general feedback is now handled at http://discourse.jabref.org.
Thanks!
-->

**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently there are only 2 options for "Read Status":  "Read" and "Skimmed". I would like to propose 2 additional statuses: "In Progress" (or "Reading") and "Queued" (or "Up Next").

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Add "In Progress" and "Queued" as two new options for "Read status".

**Additional context**
I think both of these options are worthwhile, especially for when we have hundreds/thousands of bib-entries.

*Case for "In Progress"*: If I'm in the middle of reading a paper and I want to quickly look up another paper and then I get distracted, I am extremely likely to forget what paper I was just reading. Or if it's a long article that takes a few days and I can't read it one day after another. Having an "In Progress" option would allow me to just note that this one I started, but haven't yet completed reading/skimming yet and so I should probably go back to this at some point.

*Case for "Queued"* As I'm reading an article, sometimes it cites another article and I want to read that one. I can then mark that article as "Queued" so that I know, I want to read this next. This allows for better navigation and doesn't require me to keep track on paper which paper I want to look at next.

The two options combined are also very beneficial as it allows jumping between my queue and in progress in order to not have to constantly search for things (when making auto-generated groups).

summary: Add dropdown for selecting citation key pattern

description.text:
There should be a drop-down/running list of matching suggestions available with at least the [special field markers](https://docs.jabref.org/setup/citationkeypatterns#special-field-markers) when selecting a citation key pattern.
This should reflect in both library properties as well as preferences.
The matching will involve search (filtering, to be more precise), so the list of available special field markers should be populated and kept somewhere (if not already done - as far as I am aware, we do only a case-by-case matching with the input pattern string<sup>[1]</sup>, and the generator **fails** without warning if no pattern matches<sup>[2]</sup> - no citation key is generated<sup>[3]</sup>).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5fff0465-dfe1-44d2-a60b-271ab46a9bae)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a99524b-14e9-43cc-ae46-fad9e5f8f3d1)

This will enhance UX greatly, as it will take away some load to read the documentation again if a user forgets the pattern marker, and also bring in some convenience for not having to press the enter key every time (as in, the click for selecting a sugegstion/drop-down item should be enough).


**Some more context:**
- <sup>[1]</sup> One can find the patterns in [`BracketedPattern.java`](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/19df622d4a0a057d5d3cedaaf4677527612de8e7/src/main/java/org/jabref/logic/citationkeypattern/BracketedPattern.java#L351)
- <sup>[2]</sup> In case of a single field marker. In case of compound patterns consisting of a combination of field markers, any one of them being invalid renders only THAT part invalid. The citation key is then partially generated as per the valid components only. E.g. `[auth]_[yger]` can generate `Corti_` as a key instead of `Corti_2011`.
- <sup>[3]</sup> Ideally, there should be a warning notification in that case - either while pressing enter after providing such a pattern, or on trying to generate a citation key using it (on empty string), or preferably both.

summary: Add a preference for the "Copy to" option to include or exclude cross-reference entries

description.text:
**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**

While copying entries via the "Copy to" option, if I have chosen to include them and selected the "Do not ask again" option, there is no way to change my preference later.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

There should be an option in Preferences > General tab to modify this setting, similar to the delete confirmation dialog option.

- Add an option in the Preferences > General tab.
- It should say, "Include cross-referenced entries when using the 'Copy To' option"

**Additional context**

You can refer to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12374
As shown, add another one below this in the same way.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9e3a758-d85d-4675-84ec-2693e80b1d77)

summary: "Copy To" option does not follow the preferences

description.text:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

Windows

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

There is a bug in the 'Copy To' option. Even after setting it to include cross-referenced entries in the dialog box, it does not include them the next time.

1. Copy an entry using the Copy To option.
2. When prompted, choose to include the cross-referenced entries and select "Do not ask again".
3. Copy another entry again using the Copy To option.
